---
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
      - text: |
          \input{SOP Formatting/PDF Formatting Script.tex}
highlight-style: ../../_styles/idle-fingers-light.theme
csl: ../nature.csl
---

\clearpage
\null
\thispagestyle{empty}
\newpage
\setcounter{page}{1}

The purpose of this document is to outline the contents and ongoing maintenance procedure of the Mukherjee Lab website.

**Developer:** Howard Baik, M.S.

**Authors:**

- Shelby Golden, M.S.

**Date Effective:** April 24<sup>th</sup>, 2026

**Date Updated:** N/A


\tableofcontents
\newpage

# Important Links

- [Website](https://ysph-dsde.github.io/mukherjee-lab/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ysph-dsde/mukherjee-lab)
- SharePoint (located in "DSDE - Core/General/Media & Communications/Website/Mukherjee Lab")

The project SharePoint folder contains additional links and files, including backups of CV, headshot images, and the new lab member survey link.

# Codebase File Structure

\vspace{-2ex}

\begin{flushleft}
\texttt{mukherjee-lab/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- \_extensions/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- quarto-ext/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- schochastics/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- \_styles/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- Yale\_typeface/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- code/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- SOP Formatting/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- content/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- cv/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- images/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- banner-images/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- headshots-alumnae/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- headshots-people/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- docs/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- renv/}
\end{flushleft}

# Maintaining the Website

Page content is managed through the corresponding `*.qmd` files. Refer to existing files for guidance on content organization and formatting conventions.

A GitHub Actions workflow, defined in "`.github`/workflows/`publish.yml`", automatically republishes the website when new content is pushed to the repository. It also includes a scheduled trigger that republishes the site once a month.

The `publications-preprints.qmd` file is self-maintaining — it uses an R script to automatically scrape publications from PubMed and requires no manual updates. The publications page is refreshed every time the website is republished, whether triggered by a push or a scheduled GitHub Action.

## Publishing Manually

To publish the site manually, follow the instructions below. This process will not interfere with the existing GitHub Actions.

\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
\small \textbf{Command-Line Interface} \normalsize\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}

```{.bash}
cd "FILE-PATH/mukherjee-lab/"
git add .
git commit -m "<message>"
git push
quarto publish gh-pages
```

\vspace{-5.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}


## Adding New Members

1. Send the "Lab Members" Qualtrics survey to the new lab member. The survey link can be found in the group's SharePoint folder. To edit the survey, navigate to your Yale Qualtrics account. Only designated collaborators have editing access.

2. Once the new lab member has completed the survey, their response will appear under the "Data & Analysis" tab in Qualtrics. This page is visible to survey collaborators only.

3. Click the "$\cdots$" to the right of the survey response to view the new member's details. From here, you can also download their CV and headshot.

4. After downloading the CV and headshot, rename the files to match the new member's name (e.g., `bhramar-mukherjee.pdf`).

5. Upload a copy of each file to the respective SharePoint folders as a backup. Then, move the remaining copies to the "content/cv" and "content/images/headshots-people" directories.

6. Add the new lab member to the "People/Current Members" page by copying and pasting their provided details to "pages/`people.qmd`".

7. **OPTIONAL:** Run `quarto preview` to verify that all file references, links, and content styling render correctly.

8. Commit and push your changes. This will automatically trigger a republish of the website. If manual republishing is needed, refer to the instructions in the previous section.


# Maintaining the SOP


This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) uses a preconfigured LaTeX formatting script. The report `*.qmd` and formatting files are located in "code/Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/."

If you are updating the document, please add your name to the **Authors** field and update the **Date Updated** field accordingly. The LaTeX formatting script will automatically include the date the document was rendered at the top of the page.

To preview a rendered version of the document, comment out the following line in the website's `_quarto.yml`:

\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
\small \textbf{\texttt{\_quarto.yml}} \normalsize\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}

```{.yml}
- "!code/Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/SOP.qmd"
```

\vspace{-5.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}


This line prevents the SOP from being rendered by the GitHub Actions workflow. Once you have finished editing and previewing, uncomment the line to avoid errors with the GitHub Actions workflow.

Once you have finished editing the document render it, move the resulting `*.pdf` to the project's root directory, then commit and push your changes.
